# Wallet Audit: Chase Sapphire Preferred + Amex Gold

## 💰 Annual Cost

| Card | Annual Fee |
|---|---|
| Chase Sapphire Preferred | $95 |
| Amex Gold | $250 |
| **Total** | **$345/yr** |

## 📈 Earning Map

Best card for each major spend category:

1. Dining — Amex Gold (4x vs 3x)
2. US supermarkets — Amex Gold (4x vs 1x)
3. Travel (portal) — Chase Sapphire Preferred (5x via Chase Travel)
4. Travel (flights) — Amex Gold (3x vs 2x)
5. Streaming — Chase Sapphire Preferred (3x vs 1x)
6. Online grocery — Chase Sapphire Preferred (3x vs 1x)
7. All other — Chase Sapphire Preferred (1x = 1.25 cpp via portal vs 1x MR)

## 🏷️ Credits Stack

| Credit | Card | Amount |
|---|---|---|
| Hotel credit | Chase Sapphire Preferred | $50/yr |
| Dining credit | Amex Gold | $120/yr ($10/mo) |
| Uber credit | Amex Gold | $120/yr ($10/mo) |
| **Total credits** | | **$290/yr** |

## 🔁 Overlap

1. Dining — both cards earn bonus rates; Amex Gold wins (4x vs 3x), making CSP's 3x dining redundant
2. Travel — both earn bonus rates on travel purchases; use CSP for portal bookings, Amex Gold for direct flights
3. Trip insurance — both offer trip delay/cancellation coverage

## 🕳️ Gaps

1. Flat-rate non-bonus spend — neither card exceeds 1x on non-category spend; a 2% cash back card would fill this
2. Lounge access — neither card includes lounge access
3. Cell phone protection — neither card offers it

## 📋 Confidence Notes

- Fixture only. Not live card data.
